content:
type: DRAFT
files: src/PIL/Image.py
region: save() method
code:     def save(
        self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True
        elif fp == sys.stdout:
            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        backup = params.pop("backup", False)
        backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")
        dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        if backup and filename and os.path.exists(filename):
            shutil.copy2(filename, os.fspath(filename) + backup_suffix)

        if dry_run:
            save_handler(self, io.BytesIO(), filename)
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
            return

        created = False
        if open_fp:
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp:
            fp.close()
note: Merged save() with backup/backup_suffix (mine) + dry_run (yours) params popped from params dict; backup copies existing file via shutil.copy2 before write path; dry_run runs save_handler against io.BytesIO() and returns early, skipping the open_fp/created/write block. Need shutil and io imported. Please confirm dry_run behavior matches your intent or send adjustments.

def save(
        self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True
        elif fp == sys.stdout:
            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        backup = params.pop("backup", False)
        backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")
        dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        if backup and filename and os.path.exists(filename):
            shutil.copy2(filename, os.fspath(filename) + backup_suffix)

        if dry_run:
            save_handler(self, io.BytesIO(), filename)
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
            return

        created = False
        if open_fp:
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp:
            fp.close()

files: src/PIL/Image.py

code:     def save(
        self,
        fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes],
        format: str | None = None,
        *,
        dry_run: bool = False,
        backup: bool = False,
        backup_suffix: str = ".bak",
        **params: Any,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param dry_run: If true, validates that the image can be saved
           without actually writing to ``fp`` or the filesystem.
        :param backup: If true, and a file already exists at the target
           filename, copies it to a backup file before overwriting it.
        :param backup_suffix: The suffix appended to the filename to
           create the backup file when ``backup`` is true.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True
        elif fp == sys.stdout:
            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        if dry_run:
            dry_run_fp = io.BytesIO()
            try:
                save_handler(self, dry_run_fp, filename)
            finally:
                try:
                    del self.encoderinfo
                except AttributeError:
                    pass
            return

        created = False
        if open_fp:
            if backup and os.path.exists(filename):
                shutil.copy2(filename, os.fspath(filename) + backup_suffix)
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp:
            fp.close()

def save(
        self,
        fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes],
        format: str | None = None,
        *,
        dry_run: bool = False,
        backup: bool = False,
        backup_suffix: str = ".bak",
        **params: Any,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the

        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param dry_run: If true, validates that the image can be saved
           without actually writing to ``fp`` or the filesystem.
        :param backup: If true, and a file already exists at the target
           filename, copies it to a backup file before overwriting it.
        :param backup_suffix: The suffix appended to the filename to
           create the backup file when ``backup`` is true.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image

             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image

             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True

            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass

            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        if dry_run:
            dry_run_fp = io.BytesIO()
            try:
                save_handler(self, dry_run_fp, filename)
            finally:
                try:
                    del self.encoderinfo
                except AttributeError:
                    pass
            return

        created = False
        if open_fp:
            if backup and os.path.exists(filename):
                shutil.copy2(filename, os.fspath(filename) + backup_suffix)
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass

            try:

            except AttributeError:

files: src/PIL/Image.py
region: save() method
code:     def save(
        self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])

           Additionally, the following keyword-only options are supported:

           * ``dry_run`` (bool): If ``True``, the image is fully processed for
             saving (including format conversion, compression, etc.) but the
             result is discarded and nothing is written to disk. Any exception
             that would normally occur during saving is still raised, making
             this useful for validating that an image can be saved in a given
             format. Defaults to ``False``.
           * ``backup`` (bool): If ``True`` and the destination file already
             exists, a copy of the existing file is made before it is
             overwritten. Defaults to ``False``.
           * ``backup_suffix`` (str): The suffix appended to the destination
             filename to form the backup filename. Only used when ``backup``
             is ``True``. Defaults to ``".bak"``.
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True
        elif fp == sys.stdout:
            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        backup = params.pop("backup", False)
        backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")
        dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        if backup and filename and os.path.exists(filename):
            shutil.copy2(filename, os.fspath(filename) + backup_suffix)

        if dry_run:
            try:
                save_handler(self, io.BytesIO(), filename)
            finally:
                try:
                    del self.encoderinfo
                except AttributeError:
                    pass
            return

        created = False
        if open_fp:
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp:
            fp.close()
note: Agreed. Will write this verbatim. Need imports: shutil and io must be present at top of Image.py.

def save(
        self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])

           Additionally, the following keyword-only options are supported:

           * ``dry_run`` (bool): If ``True``, the image is fully processed for
             saving (including format conversion, compression, etc.) but the
             result is discarded and nothing is written to disk. Any exception
             that would normally occur during saving is still raised, making
             this useful for validating that an image can be saved in a given
             format. Defaults to ``False``.
           * ``backup`` (bool): If ``True`` and the destination file already
             exists, a copy of the existing file is made before it is
             overwritten. Defaults to ``False``.
           * ``backup_suffix`` (str): The suffix appended to the destination
             filename to form the backup filename. Only used when ``backup``
             is ``True``. Defaults to ``".bak"``.
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True
        elif fp == sys.stdout:
            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        backup = params.pop("backup", False)
        backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")
        dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        if backup and filename and os.path.exists(filename):
            shutil.copy2(filename, os.fspath(filename) + backup_suffix)

        if dry_run:
            try:
                save_handler(self, io.BytesIO(), filename)
            finally:
                try:
                    del self.encoderinfo
                except AttributeError:
                    pass
            return

        created = False
        if open_fp:
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp:
            fp.close()

code:     def save(
        self,
        fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes],
        format: str | None = None,
        *,
        dry_run: bool = False,
        backup: bool = False,
        backup_suffix: str = ".bak",
        **params: Any,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param dry_run: If true, validates that the image can be saved
           without actually writing to ``fp`` or the filesystem.
        :param backup: If true, and a file already exists at the target
           filename, copies it to a backup file before overwriting it.
        :param backup_suffix: The suffix appended to the filename to
           create the backup file when ``backup`` is true.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True
        elif fp == sys.stdout:
            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        if dry_run:
            dry_run_fp = io.BytesIO()
            try:
                save_handler(self, dry_run_fp, filename)
            finally:
                try:
                    del self.encoderinfo
                except AttributeError:
                    pass
            return

        created = False
        if open_fp:
            if backup and os.path.exists(filename):
                shutil.copy2(filename, os.fspath(filename) + backup_suffix)
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp:
            fp.close()

                pass
        if open_fp:
            fp.close()

type: AGREE
files: src/PIL/Image.py
region: save() method (signature + body)
code:     def save(
        self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])

           Additionally, the following keyword-only options are supported:

           * ``dry_run`` (bool): If ``True``, the image is fully processed for
             saving (including format conversion, compression, etc.) but the
             result is discarded and nothing is written to disk. Any exception
             that would normally occur during saving is still raised, making
             this useful for validating that an image can be saved in a given
             format. Defaults to ``False``.
           * ``backup`` (bool): If ``True`` and the destination file already
             exists, a copy of the existing file is made before it is
             overwritten. Defaults to ``False``.
           * ``backup_suffix`` (str): The suffix appended to the destination
             filename to form the backup filename. Only used when ``backup``
             is ``True``. Defaults to ``".bak"``.
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

        filename: str | bytes = ""
        open_fp = False
        if is_path(fp):
            filename = os.fspath(fp)
            open_fp = True
        elif fp == sys.stdout:
            try:
                fp = sys.stdout.buffer
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if not filename and hasattr(fp, "name") and is_path(fp.name):
            # only set the name for metadata purposes
            filename = os.fspath(fp.name)

        preinit()

        filename_ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
        ext = filename_ext.decode() if isinstance(filename_ext, bytes) else filename_ext

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]
            except KeyError as e:
                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        backup = params.pop("backup", False)
        backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")
        dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)
        self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, "encoderinfo", {}), **params}
        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
        if save_all or (
            save_all is None
            and params.get("append_images")
            and format.upper() in SAVE_ALL
        ):
            save_handler = SAVE_ALL[format.upper()]
        else:
            save_handler = SAVE[format.upper()]

        if backup and filename and os.path.exists(filename):
            shutil.copy2(filename, os.fspath(filename) + backup_suffix)

        if dry_run:
            try:
                save_handler(self, io.BytesIO(), filename)
            finally:
                try:
                    del self.encoderinfo
                except AttributeError:
                    pass
            return

        created = False
        if open_fp:
            created = not os.path.exists(filename)
            if params.get("append", False):
                # Open also for reading ("+"), because TIFF save_all
                # writer needs to go back and edit the written data.
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "r+b")
            else:
                fp = builtins.open(filename, "w+b")
        else:
            fp = cast(IO[bytes], fp)

        try:
            save_handler(self, fp, filename)
        except Exception:
            if open_fp:
                fp.close()
            if created:
                try:
                    os.remove(filename)
                except PermissionError:
                    pass
            raise
        finally:
            try:
                del self.encoderinfo
            except AttributeError:
                pass
        if open_fp:
            fp.close()

        self, fp: StrOrBytesPath | IO[bytes], format: str | None = None, **params: Any
    ) -> None:
        """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the

        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])

           * ``dry_run`` (bool): If ``True``, the image is fully processed for

        preinit()

        if not format:
            if ext not in EXTENSION:
                init()
            try:
                format = EXTENSION[ext]

                msg = f"unknown file extension: {ext}"
                raise ValueError(msg) from e

        # may mutate self!
        self._ensure_mutable()

        save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)
        backup = params.pop("backup", False)
        backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")
        dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)

        self.encoderconfig: tuple[Any, ...] = ()

        if format.upper() not in SAVE:
            init()
